item.title: Junior Bula Boys reach OFC U-16 final, seal U-17 World Cup berth
item.description: Junior Bula Boys reach OFC U-16 final, seal U-17 World Cup berth

item.title: Govt and Chinese Embassy support Fiji U18 girls' historic World Championship campaign
item.description: The team presented their iTautau today, seeking the Government's blessing as they depart tonight for Romania. Speaking during the traditional ceremony at the Ministry of Youth and Sports, Minister for Housing Maciu Nalumisa acknowledged the team and officials for presenting the iTautau ceremony, saying it is an important part of Fijian culture and tradition.
item.content:encoded:
The team presented their iTautau today, seeking the Government's blessing as they depart tonight for Romania.

Speaking during the traditional ceremony at the Ministry of Youth and Sports, Minister for Housing Maciu Nalumisa acknowledged the team and officials for presenting the iTautau ceremony, saying it is an important part of Fijian culture and tradition.

Nalumisa says the ceremony carries the Government's blessing and that blessings will follow when traditions are observed in the right way.

He says Fiji's identity and cultural values define who Fijians are, and the players must uphold those values when they travel overseas.

The Minister reminded the Under-18 girls that they will not only be representing Fiji but also their individual families, urging them to make the country proud.

Nalumisa also acknowledged the sacrifices made by the players and their families to prepare for the tournament and encouraged the team to give their best, regardless of the result.

Quoting former United States President John F. Kennedy, Nalumisa told the players, "Ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country," encouraging them to proudly serve Fiji.

Meanwhile, Head of Delegation Lusiana Rokoura says the team is excited and eager to compete after months of preparation.

Rokoura says the girls qualified for the World Championship after winning the Oceania Youth Championships in the Cook Islands last October and are looking forward to testing themselves against some of the world's best teams.

She says Fiji has been drawn alongside world number four France, Egypt and Croatia, and while the challenge will be tough, the team's goal is to become the best versions of themselves and gain valuable international experience.

Rokoura says most of the players are still in high school, making the opportunity even more significant for their development.

She says the team will spend a few days in Hong Kong to acclimatise before travelling to Romania, adding that while the players are nervous, they are equally determined to make history.

Rokoura thanked the Government for providing funding through the Office of the Prime Minister Grant, saying it ensured the 12-member team could proudly represent Fiji at the World Championship.

Rokoura says without the Government's support, the historic campaign would not have been possible.

She also acknowledged the Embassy of the People's Republic of China in Fiji for its timely financial assistance, saying it enabled the team's four-day stopover in Hong Kong to complete visa processing before travelling to Romania.

Rokoura says the Embassy's support came at a critical time and gave the team hope to keep their dream of competing on the world stage alive.

Looking beyond the tournament, Rokoura says Fiji Handball plans to strengthen the sport through more awareness programmes across the country, increased school competitions and a stronger pathway from grassroots to the national team.

She says Fiji's participation at the World Championship is a historic milestone not only for the country but for the Pacific region, and the team is honoured to represent both Fiji and the region on the world stage.

After meeting Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka at the Office of the Prime Minister today, Assistant Minister for Foreign Affairs Lenora Qereqeretabua stopped by to wish her cousin, Rokoura, and the Fiji Under-18 girls' team all the best ahead of their historic campaign at the IHF Women's Youth World Championship in Romania.

Qereqeretabua also gave a donation to support the team's preparations and participation in the tournament.

The Fiji Under-18 girls will begin their historic IHF Women's Youth World Championship campaign against world number four France on 29th July.

They will then face hosts Croatia on 30th July before taking on Egypt on the 1st of August in their final pool match.

Fiji is making its first appearance at the tournament, becoming the first Pacific nation to compete at the IHF Women's Youth World Championship.

item.title: Young aiming to break personal best at first Commonwealth Games
item.description: The 26-year-old from Nakaseleka in Kadavu recently equalled her 100 metres personal best at the Oceania Championships and is determined to go one step further at the Games. Speaking to fijivillage Sports, Young says training has been the toughest it has ever been, but having a supportive group of athletes has helped everyone push through the demanding sessions.
item.content:encoded:
The 26-year-old from Nakaseleka in Kadavu recently equalled her 100 metres personal best at the Oceania Championships and is determined to go one step further at the Games.

Speaking to fijivillage Sports, Young says training has been the toughest it has ever been, but having a supportive group of athletes has helped everyone push through the demanding sessions.

She says the team constantly encourages one another, making the preparation more enjoyable.

Although this will be her first Commonwealth Games, Young has previously represented Fiji at the Commonwealth Youth Games.

She says competing against world-class athletes will be a big challenge, but believes racing against the best will bring out the best in her.

Young will compete in the women's 100 metres and 200 metres.

She also paid tribute to her training partner, Erol Qaqa, saying his positive attitude, work ethic and energy during training continue to inspire and motivate her, especially on the toughest days.

Young says she is taking her preparation one training session at a time while remaining focused on achieving a new personal best in Glasgow.

The Glasgow Commonwealth Games will kick off this Friday and end on the 2nd of August.

The women's 100 metres event is scheduled for Monday, 27th July , from 10am to 1pm, while the women's 200 metres will be held on Wednesday, 29th July , also from 10am to 1pm.

item.title: History comes home as Fijian Drua Women host first Super Rugby Women’s grand final in Ba
item.description: History will be made this Saturday when the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women host the Super Rugby Women's Grand Final on home soil for the first time, with the club calling on fans from across the country to be part of the occasion. Fijian Drua Head of Commercial and Marketing Shane Hussein says the final is not only a milestone for the club but for Fiji, as it marks the first time the country will host
item.content:encoded:
History will be made this Saturday when the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women host the Super Rugby Women's Grand Final on home soil for the first time, with the club calling on fans from across the country to be part of the occasion.

Fijian Drua Head of Commercial and Marketing Shane Hussein says the final is not only a milestone for the club but for Fiji, as it marks the first time the country will host the competition's decider.

Hussein says although the Drua finished at the top of the table in 2022, they were not eligible to host the grand final because the competition was then exclusively Australian.

He says Rugby Australia has since recognised the success of the Drua Women, allowing the team to earn the right to host the final by finishing at the top of the table and winning their semi-final.

Insert: Shane - Be part of history July 22

He says the final will be played as a double-header, with the Ba and Lautoka women's teams featuring in the curtain-raiser, making the venue's infrastructure an important consideration.

Hussein stressed that while Lautoka has become the Drua Women's fortress, the club wanted to give Ba the opportunity to host such a significant match and gain experience staging major rugby events.

The grand final kicks off at 1pm on Saturday at 4R Stadium in Ba, with tickets priced at $5, $10 and $15.

#SPORTS | Be part of history this Saturday! Fijian Drua Head of Commercial and Marketing Shane Hussein is calling on fans to pack 4R Stadium in Ba as the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women host the Super Rugby Women's Grand Final on home soil for the first time. 🎥 Video: Temalesi Vono, fijivillage News Have a news tip or captured something happening in your community? Send your photos and videos to us via Facebook Messenger or Instagram.

item.title: Junior Bula Boys face Solomon Islands for place at 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup
item.description: The Fiji U-16 side is just one win away from securing a place at a third consecutive FIFA U-17 World Cup as it takes on the Solomon Islands in the semi-final of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship in Papua New Guinea in the next hour. A victory will not only book Fiji a place in the tournament final but also secure qualification for the 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup, continuing the Junior Bula Boys' remark
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji U-16 side is just one win away from securing a place at a third consecutive FIFA U-17 World Cup as it takes on the Solomon Islands in the semi-final of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship in Papua New Guinea in the next hour.

A victory will not only book Fiji a place in the tournament final but also secure qualification for the 2027 FIFA U-17 World Cup, continuing the Junior Bula Boys' remarkable run on the world stage.

Fiji made its FIFA U-17 World Cup debut last year before qualifying again for this year's tournament, and a victory over the Solomon Islands would secure the Junior Bula Boys a place at a third consecutive FIFA U-17 World Cup.

Fiji Football Association Vice-President and Head of Delegation, Ritesh Pratap says the players understand the significance of the occasion.

He says the players have worked incredibly hard to reach this stage , and everyone involved with the team is proud of what they have achieved so far.

He is urging all Fijians to continue supporting and praying for the team as they take on the Solomon Islands in this crucial semi-final.

Fiji heads into the semi-final having scored 10 goals without conceding any .

The Junior Bula Boys beat New Caledonia 2–1, thrashed Samoa 5–0 and defeated Papua New Guinea 3–0 in their final pool match to top the standings.

Fiji will play the Solomon Islands in the semi-final at the PNG Football Stadium at 6pm, while New Zealand will face Papua New Guinea in the other semi-final at 9.30pm.

The final will be played at 9.30pm this Saturday.

item.title: Lelean to face RKS for Lutubula Trophy
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 22/07/2026 The prestigious Lutubula Trophy will be up for grabs this weekend as favourites Lelean Memorial School take on Ratu Kadavulevu School in the final of the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Under-18 Secondary Schools Rugby Union competition.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 22/07/2026

The prestigious Lutubula Trophy will be up for grabs this weekend as favourites Lelean Memorial School take on Ratu Kadavulevu School in the final of the Vodafone Deans Eastern Zone Under-18 Secondary Schools Rugby Union competition.

The Lutubula Trophy , once regarded as the symbol of dominance among Eastern Zone rugby powerhouses such as Queen Victoria School, Ratu Kadavulevu School and Lelean Memorial School, also serves as part of the qualification pathway to the Vodafone Deans competition.

Lelean heads into the final unbeaten this season and has been the dominant force in the Eastern Zone competition.

Defending Lutubula Trophy champions Queen Victoria School surrendered the title after a closely contested semi-final loss to Ratu Kadavulevu School last weekend.

RKS, the reigning Vodafone Deans Under-18 champions, will be looking to add another trophy to their cabinet while also securing a direct pathway to the national quarter-finals.

In the other finals, Queen Victoria School will face Ratu Kadavulevu School in the Under-14, Under-16 and Under-17 grades. Lelean Memorial School will take on Ratu Kadavulevu School in the Under-15 and Under-19 finals.

The Eastern Zone finals will be held at HFC Bank Stadium this Saturday.

item.title: Legge sticks with winning squad for Super W Final in Ba this Saturday
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 22/07/2026 McDonald's Fiji Drua Women Head Coach Mike Legge has named an unchanged 23-member squad for Saturday's Super Rugby Women’s Grand Final against the NSW Waratahs in Ba.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 22/07/2026

McDonald's Fiji Drua Women Head Coach Mike Legge has named an unchanged 23-member squad for Saturday's Super Rugby Women’s Grand Final against the NSW Waratahs in Ba.

History will be made this weekend as Fiji hosts the Super Rugby Women’s Grand Final for the first time, highlighting the remarkable growth of women’s rugby and the passionate support of the Drua Nation.

Karalaini Naisewa will make her 31st appearance for the Drua, joining Tiana Robanakadavu and Keleni Marawa in the front row.

Mereoni Nakesa and Carletta Yee will start in the second row, while Nunia Daunimoala and Salaseini Railumu retain their places at blindside and openside flanker respectively.

Captain Kolora Lomani will partner Varanisese Qoro in the halves, with Aqela Raitubu and Atelaite Buna on the wings.

Josivini Naihamu and Merewairita Neivosa will combine in midfield, while Litiana Vueti starts at fullback.

On the bench, season captain Bitila Tawake provides cover at hooker, with Zipporah Sorokacika and Keri Lawavou the reserve props.

Karavaki Lutumaibau covers the locks, Alfreda Fisher provides loose forward cover, Evivi Senikarivi backs up the halves, Repeka Tove covers the midfield, and Verenaisi Ditavutu completes the matchday squad as back-three cover.

The Fijian Drua Women will host the Waratahs at 1pm Saturday at 4R Stadium at Govind Park, Ba.

item.title: Kumar calls for Fijian support ahead of Junior Bula Boys' semi-final clash
item.description: Junior Bula Boys right midfielder Navitesh Kumar is calling on Fijians to continue backing the team as they prepare to face the Solomon Islands in the OFC U-16 Men's Championship semi-final today. Following their final pool match, the Labasa youngster praised his teammates for their commitment, saying they have fought hard and given their all in every match.
item.content:encoded:
Junior Bula Boys right midfielder Navitesh Kumar is calling on Fijians to continue backing the team as they prepare to face the Solomon Islands in the OFC U-16 Men's Championship semi-final today.

Following their final pool match, the Labasa youngster praised his teammates for their commitment, saying they have fought hard and given their all in every match.

He also acknowledged the efforts of the coaching and technical staff, thanking them for their work behind the scenes.

Kumar also thanked the families and supporters who have been following the team's journey from Fiji.

The Junior Bula Boys beat New Caledonia 2–1, thrashed Samoa 5–0 and defeated PNG 3–0 in their final group match to top the pool.

Fiji will play the Solomon Islands in the semi-final at the PNG Football Stadium at 6pm, while New Zealand will face PNG in the other semi-final at 9.30pm.

The final will be played at 9.30pm this Saturday.

The winner and runner-up will qualify for the FIFA U-17 World Cup 2027.

item.title: Lomani’s brilliance shines as Drua Women prepare for final showdown
item.description: Kolora Lomani is a special player with plenty more to offer, with her natural talent and skills were on full display when she scor
